meta.title: CodeBite — Master Coding Through Deliberate Practice

hero.description: Anders Ericsson's research shows that experts don't just practice more — they practice differently. CodeBite applies those principles to coding interviews.

method.title: Two Ways to Practice
method.subtitle: Most people default to the left. Experts choose the right.
naivePoints: Pick random problems from a list
Solve easy ones to feel productive
Hit a plateau and give up
codebitePoints: Work at the edge of your ability
Get immediate feedback on every attempt

science.title: The Science Behind CodeBite
science.subtitle: Every feature is grounded in decades of research on how humans actually develop expertise.

plateau.title: Most developers plateau after 1–2 years
plateau.description: Ericsson found that experience alone doesn't equal expertise. Without deliberate challenge, skills stagnate. CodeBite keeps you at your growth edge.
plateau.label: plateau

forgetting.title: You forget 70% within 24 hours
forgetting.description: Without review, knowledge fades fast. Our spaced repetition engine resurfaces problems at optimal intervals — turning short-term solving into long-term mastery.

feedback.title: 3.5× faster improvement with immediate feedback
feedback.description: Ericsson's research shows that immediate, targeted feedback is the single biggest accelerator of skill development. Every submission gives you instant, detailed results.

cta.quote: Ericsson's research across chess, music, and medicine found that deliberate practice is the #1 predictor of elite performance — not talent, not years of experience.
cta.quoteHighlight: deliberate practice is the #1 predictor of elite performance
cta.source: — Peak: Secrets from the New Science of Expertise
cta.heading: Stop passive learning. Start deliberate practice.
cta.subtitle: Join developers building real problem-solving skills.
